parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03431-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Parse CSV Contents

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming document records to a stable schema

Next: Load Rows into Airtable

### Load Rows into Airtable

Upserts into Airtable's 'Documents' table, matched on `documentId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Twilio

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Issue invoices with HTTP / REST API and Airtable and log the results

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Issue invoices with HTTP / REST API and Airtable and log the results. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual contracts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Load Rows into Airtable → Tag Pipeline Metadata → Stamp Processing Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating contracts in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Airtable PAT; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your contracts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
